title: 10 best herbal essential oils for ulcerative colitis
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for ulcerative colitis are a natural and effective approach to managing the symptoms of this chronic inflammatory bowel disease. These potent plant extracts have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation, ease abdominal pain, and promote healing in the digestive tract. Examples of herbal essential oils that show promise include peppermint oil, which can calm cramps and spasms, and lavender oil, which can reduce stress and anxiety. By incorporating these oils into aromatherapy, bath routines, or topical applications, individuals with ulcerative colitis may experience improved digestion, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically or ingested as a dietary supplement, peppermint oil can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea. The menthol content also relaxes the muscles in the colon, reducing spasms and cramps associated with ulcerative colitis. Additionally, peppermint oil's calming effects on the gut can promote a healthy balance of gut bacteria, further contributing to its therapeutic benefits for individuals suffering from this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping associated with Ulcerative Colitis.
Antispasmodic properties: The oil's ability to relax smooth muscle spasms can help to reduce bowel movements frequency, urgency, and pain caused by Ulcerative Colitis.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint essential oil contains antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and oxidative stress associated with Ulcerative Colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and volatile compounds, peppermint roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe digestive issues associated with ulcerative colitis.

Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), a condition often linked to ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves at peak potency for optimal aroma and flavor.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the leaves using a still or a distillation apparatus to release the oil.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a separate container after distillation is complete.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the development of ulcers and bowel damage. Additionally, ginger essential oil has been found to increase mucus production, protecting the colon lining from irritation and reducing symptoms like abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory compound, gingerol, reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea associated with ulcerative colitis.
Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant properties protect the colon from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.
Digestive stimulant property: The essential oil stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating, gas, and constipation often experienced by individuals with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, the underground stem of the ginger plant, contains active compounds that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce pain and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots carefully selecting those with no signs of disease or damage weighing at least 2 pounds.
Wash the harvested ginger roots thoroughly with filtered water removing any dirt debris or bacteria.
Steam distill the washed ginger roots using a commercial still or a homemade setup maintaining temperatures between 140-160 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using cheesecloth or coffee filters ensuring no impurities or sediment remain weighing approximately 1 ounce per pound of ginger root used.
Store the final zingiber officinale essential oil in dark glass bottles tightly sealed away from direct sunlight heat or oxygen at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the colon, which is a hallmark symptom of ulcerative colitis. Additionally, curcumin has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thereby decreasing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut. This natural remedy may provide relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and fatigue associated with ulcerative colitis, offering hope for those seeking alternative treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcuminoids, can reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms like diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric essential oil help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to Ulcerative Colitis development and progression.

Gut-soothing: Turmeric essential oil's active compounds, such as curcumin and volatile oils, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the gut, reducing symptoms like rectal bleeding, itching, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
Rhizomes: The primary source of curcumin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve clinical outcomes in patients with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality curcuma longa roots and dry them in a warm place for at least 7 days.
Crush dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 250 grams of the powdered root with 750 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Steep mixture for 14 to 21 days, shaking jar daily to facilitate extraction, before straining and discarding solids.
Transfer clear liquid extract to dark glass bottle and store at room temperature away from light for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The calming effects of lavender oil also help to alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for ulcerative colitis flares. Additionally, lavender's antispasmodic properties can help to reduce muscle spasms in the colon, providing relief from cramping and diarrhea associated with the condition. Overall, using English lavender essential oils as a natural remedy can provide significant relief for those suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the colon, which is a common symptom of Ulcerative Colitis.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of English Lavender essential oil can help protect the mucous membrane lining of the colon from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate Ulcerative Colitis symptoms.

Anxiolytic: English Lavender essential oil's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for Ulcerative Colitis flares and can also make symptoms worse.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in reducing inflammation and soothing symptoms associated with ulcerative colitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the English lavender plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to protect against oxidative stress and reduce inflammation in the gut.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers from healthy plants, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants, approximately 100g.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers using a clean food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or air drying method taking about 7-10 days.
Combine 50g of dried lavender flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar, shaking gently to mix and infusing for 2 weeks.
Filter the infused mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding solids and preserving the filtered liquid as the essential oil, approximately 20-25ml.
Store the obtained lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with an tight-fitting lid, kept at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The oil's flavonoids and terpenes also have antioxidant effects, protecting against oxidative stress and promoting healing in the colon lining. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties can help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and diarrhea, providing relief to those suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can help alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis such as abdominal pain and diarrhea.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the mucous membranes in the colon from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.

Gastroprotective properties: Fennel essential oil has been found to have a protective effect on the stomach and intestinal lining, reducing the risk of ulcers and inflammation associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming digestive issues associated with ulcerative colitis.
Barks: The barks of the fennel plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve gut health for people with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at the peak of potency.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or machine drying for 24 hours.
Distill the dried plant material using a steam distillation process for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the inflamed intestinal lining. The oil's natural compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms like diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping. Additionally, chamomile has been found to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can exacerbate IBD symptoms. By applying topical chamomile oil or inhaling its steam, individuals with ulcerative colitis may experience relief from their condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the colon, which can alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bleeding.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile essential oil's antioxidant property helps to protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of ulcerative colitis.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile essential oil's soothing and calming property can help to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for ulcerative colitis flares, and promote relaxation and comfort in the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with ulcerative colitis.

Flowers: The delicate petals of chamomile flowers contain apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them beneficial for ulcerative colitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomile flowers at dawn on a sunny day, collecting a minimum of 50g for infusion.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low heat for 8 hours.
Combine the dried chamomile flowers with 250ml of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a glass jar, leaving space for expansion.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and preserving the resulting essential oil in airtight dark bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The oil's antimicrobial activity also helps to eliminate harmful bacteria and other pathogens that can exacerbate symptoms of the condition. Antioxidant properties: Marjoram essential oil contains antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammatory bowel diseases like ulcerative colitis.

Gastroprotective properties: Marjoram essential oil has been found to have gastroprotective effects, which means it can help shield the stomach lining from damage and reduce the risk of ulcers and other gastrointestinal complications associated with ulcerative colitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh oregano leaves and flowers from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier.
Dry the oregano leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours or air drying for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried oregano herbs in a still using distilled water for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the distilled water and filter it through a coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the origanum majorana essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the colon and alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bitter orange peels and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Dry the peel pieces in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours or until brittle.
Steam distill 100g of dried bitter orange peel with 500ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles.
Store the citrus x aurantium essential oil in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves at peak potency during the summer months.
Steam distill the fresh leaves with water in a ratio of 10:1 using a still for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting distillate through cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.
Combine the filtered essential oil with carrier oils such as jojoba or sweet almond oil at a 5% dilution ratio for topical application.
Store the finished essential oil blend in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated at dawn.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or shade drying for 7-10 days ensuring moisture level below 20%.
Steam distill dried thymus vulgaris leaves with water in a ratio of 1:4 using a high-quality still for 90-120 minutes.
Cool and separate the essential oil from the distillation residues by centrifugation or filtration at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Bottle the extracted thymus vulgaris essential oil without additives or preservatives in dark glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and terpenes, help to reduce inflammation in the colon, which is a key symptom of the condition. Seeds: Lemon balm seeds contain antioxidants and fatty acids that help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves from organic farms or your own garden in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or by spreading them out in a single layer and allowing them to air dry for 7-10 days.
Steam-distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves with 10% water and 90% distilled steam at a temperature of 100°c to 105°c for 30-40 minutes to extract the essential oil.

Filter the distillation mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a glass vial and discard the solids, then store the filtered essential oil in an amber glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

Use the melissa officinalis essential oil topically by mixing 5-10 drops with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and applying it to the affected area up to three times a day for at least two weeks.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of rosmarinus officinalis from reputable sources.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for at least 24 hours or until crisp and brittle.
Combine one cup of dried leaves with three cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids, then store the essential oil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and stems in the early morning dew.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for distillation.
Steam-distill the chopped eucalyptus globulus mixture using 100 ml of water and a glass distillation apparatus for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and collect 10 ml of eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature.

Dry the leaves using shade or machine drying at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves in a still at 100°c for 4 hours with 200g of water.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles at 5°c.

Steam distill the cleaned pelargonium graveolens leaves in a 10:1 ratio with pure water at atmospheric pressure for 4 hours to extract the essential oils.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to separate the essential oil from the hydrosol and plant material.
Store the extracted pelargonium graveolens essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 6 months.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help alleviate inflammation and reduce oxidative stress associated with ulcerative colitis.
Rhyzomes: Containing sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds, rhyzomes exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for managing ulcerative colitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum leaves, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants, within one year of growth.
Steam distill the harvested cinnamomum verum leaves using a 10% leaf-to-water ratio and a temperature range of 100-150°f for four hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a paper coffee filter to remove any impurities, ensuring the final product has a minimum of 2% cineol content.
Mix the filtered essential oil with 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to create a blend suitable for topical application, taking approximately 30 minutes to prepare.
Store the finished cinnamomum verum essential oil blend in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature, allowing it to mature for at least six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract. The oil's antimicrobial properties also help to eliminate harmful bacteria that can exacerbate symptoms. Additionally, dill essential oil has been shown to increase digestion and alleviate abdominal cramps, bloating, and diarrhea commonly associated with ulcerative colitis. Its soothing effects on the gut lining can provide relief from inflammation and discomfort, promoting a healthier digestive system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dill essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis by decreasing prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Antioxidant: Dill essential oil is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, a common contributing factor to ulcerative colitis.

Spasmolytic: Dill essential oil has spasmolytic properties, meaning it can help relax and soothe smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which may alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, cramping, and bloating associated with ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, dill roots are believed to help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with ulcerative colitis.
Fruits: The fruits of the dill plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that may help soothe digestive issues and reduce symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh anethum graveolens leaves in early morning or evening to ensure optimal potency.
Wash the leaves with distilled water and a tablespoon of apple cider vinegar to remove dirt and impurities.
Steam distill 100 grams of washed leaves with 500 milliliters of water for 30 minutes to extract essential oil.
Combine the extracted essential oil with 10 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for blending.
Store the anethum graveolens essential oil blend in dark glass bottles at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tarragon essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as limonene and estragole, work synergistically to soothe irritated tissues, alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain and diarrhea, and promote healing of damaged mucous membranes. Additionally, tarragon essential oils may also modulate the immune response, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, leading to improved overall digestive health and quality of life for individuals suffering from ulcerative colitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tarragon essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a key characteristic of Ulcerative Colitis.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Tarragon essential oil can help protect the colon from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of Ulcerative Colitis.

Anxiolytic: Tarragon essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, common symptoms associated with Ulcerative Colitis that can exacerbate the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tarragon plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Rhyzomes: Tarragon rhyzomes are rich in volatile oils that have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including inflammatory bowel diseases like ulcerative colitis, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia dracunculus leaves at dawn or before full sun for optimal oil quality and potency.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris, then pat dry with a clean cloth.
Chop 500 grams of washed and dried artemisia dracunculus leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for steam distillation.
Place the chopped leaves in a stainless steel or glass still containing 2 liters of distilled water and heat slowly over medium flame for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and settle, then carefully decant the clear essential oil from the top layer into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves essential oils helps with ulcerative colitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the colon. The oil's potent antioxidants also work to neutralize free radicals and repair damaged cells, promoting a healthy gut environment. Additionally, clove oil's analgesic properties can help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping, providing relief from discomfort. By using cloves essential oils topically or through aromatherapy, individuals with ulcerative colitis may experience improved digestive health and reduced symptom severity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Clove essential oil's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to the development and progression of ulcerative colitis.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in clove essential oil, such as eugenol, may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the colon, which is a hallmark symptom of ulcerative colitis.

Gut-healing property: Clove essential oil has been shown to have wound-healing properties, which can aid in the repair and regeneration of damaged mucous membranes in the gut, potentially helping to alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Clove roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of ulcerative colitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of the clove plant is rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe irritated tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried syzygium aromaticum bark and grind it into a fine powder weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the powdered bark with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 1:3 ratio weighing 300 milliliters.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes to allow for decarboxylation and solubilization of bioactive compounds.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter paper into a glass bottle storing it at room temperature away from light.
Allow the oil to mature for 14 days before using it topically as a treatment for ulcerative colitis symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ulcerative colitis with herbal essential oils is to combine bergamot oil for stress relief and anti-inflammatory properties with peppermint oil's soothing digestive benefits. Apply topically after diluting with a carrier oil, or inhale the blend through steam inhalation or aromatherapy. Additionally, turmeric oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can be added to food or taken orally as a supplement to further support gut health and inflammation reduction.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ulcerative colitis that are treated with herbal essential oils are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and Crohn's disease. Certain essential oils, such as peppermint, fennel, ginger, and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain, diarrhea, constipation, and bloating. These oils may also help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
